reviews.review_title: Great, but Needs Room Practice Mode
reviews.review_description:
First, I really appreciate your quick updates. Thank you! However, I have one piece of feedback.

Auto-centring after every action is good for small spaces, but it forces players to adjust their position each time, making it hard to use.

My suggestion is to add a quick re-centre feature. For example, it would be much better if I could re-centre the view myself using a thumb double tap on both hands.
Also, the menu sometimes sinks into the wall, so it would be great if I could open it using just hand gestures.
 (2026-03-29)
—
I think this is a great app. However, I have stopped using it. The reason, like many other users, is that it cannot be practised at home.

When I follow the app and continue the movements, I am eventually asked to move into the wall.
If I reset the centre position each time, it does not help my practice, so I have no choice but to close the app.

As a suggestion, you could keep the current system as a “Dojang Mode”, which strictly requires accurate movement. In addition, you could add a “Home Mode”, where the user’s position is automatically adjusted back to the centre after each movement.

This would allow people to practise even in small rooms, while making small position adjustments as needed.

I hope the app can become something that people can use anywhere.

I’ve decided to put my 5 starts by the idea, I think this application could get more clients sure,  if you make it the work until 1,5 x 1,5 meters .. or exactly 2x2mt , in my experience. Meta quest is for enjoy at home on your bedroom , with bed at my bedroom is so difficult to job fine, my space to work is exactly 2,1 x 2,10. But the exercises go beyond this.

Thank you

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review! Regarding play area size, this app teaches the actual poomsae required for Taekwondo gradings. These are dictated by Kukkiwon, and unfortunately many require more than 2mx2m. I cannot change that without compromising on the actual poomsae.

reviews.review_description: A very clever use of VR tech. Great way to learn the patterns, and also reflect and refine movements and stances.
High quality, virtually assisted instruction.   Very useful in my training.
